description:
You are on-call as a site-reliability engineer.  
    The previous team left you a tiny *legacy* Bash program that “checks uptime”.  
    Your goal is to run that old code, archive its raw output, and then publish a minimal summary that other tools can pick up.

    Environment that already exists
    --------------------------------
    1. Directory: /home/user/legacy_tools  
       • /home/user/legacy_tools/legacy_uptime_checker.sh – **already executable**  
       • /home/user/legacy_tools/mock_ping_results.dat – static data the script reads  
       • /home/user/legacy_tools/hosts.txt – not actually used by the script but left for posterity

    2. There is **no** /home/user/logs directory yet.

    What you must deliver
    ---------------------
    A. A *raw* log file saved in **/home/user/logs/uptime_latest.log**  
       • The file must contain exactly the line-for-line console output produced when you run  
         /home/user/legacy_tools/legacy_uptime_checker.sh  
       • No extra blank lines, no header, no footer—just the four lines the script prints.

    B. A *summary* file saved in **/home/user/logs/summary_latest.log**  
       • It must be exactly four lines, each key=value on its own line, in the order shown below:  
         TOTAL_HOSTS=<integer>  
         HOSTS_UP=<integer>  
         HOSTS_DOWN=<integer>  
         TIMESTAMP=<YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS>  
       • TOTAL_HOSTS must equal the number of lines in uptime_latest.log.  
       • HOSTS_UP and HOSTS_DOWN must be the counts of the strings “UP” and “DOWN” found in uptime_latest.log.  
       • TIMESTAMP must be the date & time (local system clock) when you generated the summary, formatted literally as shown (24-hour clock, leading zeros, one space between date and time).

    C. Console proof  
       • After creating the summary file, display its contents with an appropriate command so that whoever reads your terminal session can visually confirm the four key=value lines.

    Constraints & tips
    ------------------
    • Stay completely in the user’s home area (/home/user/…). You do not need sudo.  
    • Do *not* modify the legacy Bash script or its data files.  
    • Feel free to create the /home/user/logs directory if it doesn’t exist, and ensure you have write permissions.  
    • Use any method you like to compute the counts, but the summary file has to match the format above **exactly** (no spaces around “=”).  
    • A normal, efficient shell session should need roughly 5-10 commands:  
      navigating, running the old script, moving/redirecting output, building the summary, then showing it.

    Deliverables recap
    ------------------
    1. /home/user/logs/uptime_latest.log  – raw output (4 lines)  
    2. /home/user/logs/summary_latest.log – 4-line key=value summary  
    3. Terminal output where the final step echoes the contents of summary_latest.log

    When everything looks right, you’re done.  🎉
truth:
<!--
        Files that already exist before the agent starts
    -->
    <preexisting>
        <dir path="/home/user/legacy_tools" mode="755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/legacy_tools/legacy_uptime_checker.sh" mode="755"><![CDATA[
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# === Legacy uptime checker – do not edit ===
# Reads two columns (hostname and status) from mock_ping_results.dat
# and prints the result in the format "<hostname>: <status>".

while read -r host status; do
    if [[ "$status" == "UP" ]]; then
        echo "${host}: UP"
    else
        echo "${host}: DOWN"
    fi
done < "$(dirname "$0")/mock_ping_results.dat"
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/legacy_tools/mock_ping_results.dat" mode="644"><![CDATA[
web01 UP
web02 DOWN
db01 UP
cache01 DOWN
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/legacy_tools/hosts.txt" mode="644"><![CDATA[
web01
web02
db01
cache01
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!--
        Expected artifacts after the agent completes the task
    -->
    <expected>
        <dir path="/home/user/logs" mode="755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/uptime_latest.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
web01: UP
web02: DOWN
db01: UP
cache01: DOWN
]]></file>

        <!--
            The summary file’s first three lines are deterministic;
            TIMESTAMP is checked only for format (####-##-## ##:##:##).
        -->
        <file path="/home/user/logs/summary_latest.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
TOTAL_HOSTS=4
HOSTS_UP=2
HOSTS_DOWN=2
TIMESTAMP=_ANY_VALID_TIMESTAMP_
]]></file>
    </expected>
